channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Reed Hastings is leaving Netflix after 29 years
item.description: Netflix co-founder and current chairman Reed Hastings is leaving the streaming company’s board in June to focus on "his philanthropy and other pursuits," according to a shareholder letter released alongside Netflix's Q1 earnings. Hastings has served as chairman of Netflix's board since 2023, a role he assumed after stepping down as co-CEO and promoting Greg Peters in his place."Netflix changed my life in so many ways, and my all‑time favorite memory was January 2016, when we enabled nearly the entire planet to enjoy our service," Hastings said in a statement. "My real contribution at Netflix w...

item.title: Hollywood’s First Big Budget AI-Generated Movie Is About Bitcoin, of Course
item.description: Casey Affleck and Gal Gadot star in 'Paycheck: The Movie.'

item.title: Netflix co-founder and chair Reed Hastings to leave board
item.description: Hastings helped to transform the video rental industry — first with physical, then digital, delivery.

item.title: 'Works in the dark': Scientists transform balsa wood into a solar material that stores heat and generates power 24/7
item.description: Scientists engineered balsa wood to absorb sunlight, store heat, and generate electricity in darkness using nanoscale materials and phase change technology.

item.title: White House Is Reportedly Ready to Drop Its Anthropic Beef and Embrace the Spooky New Model
item.description: Anthropic's Mythos might soon be deployed across the federal government.

item.title: A Critical Ocean Current System May Be Unraveling Faster Than We Thought
item.description: New findings foretell a looming catastrophe that would drastically alter the planet’s weather and climate.

item.title: Lana Del Rey Just Released the First Bond Theme We’ll Hear for Years
item.description: The James Bond game '007 First Light' gets a dreamy ballad from the pop star.

item.title: These are the stunning images of the Sony World Photography Awards 2026 — and there are some surprising camera choices among the winners
item.description: SWPA is one of the most prestigious global photo contests, and the 2026 instalment attracted over 430,000 from more than 200 countries. Here are the 10 category winners

item.title: Salesforce launches Headless 360 to turn its entire platform into infrastructure for AI agents
item.description: Salesforce on Wednesday unveiled the most ambitious architectural transformation in its 27-year history, introducing "Headless 360" — a sweeping initiative that exposes every capability in its platform as an API, MCP tool, or CLI command so AI agents can operate the entire system without ever opening a browser.The announcement, made at the company's annual TDX developer conference in San Francisco, ships more than 100 new tools and skills immediately available to developers. It marks a decisive response to the existential question hanging over enterprise software: In a world where AI agents ca...

item.title: Tesla Wants a $50,000 Penalty for Anyone Who Tries to Resell Its Signature Model S and X
item.description: Why would anyone want to sell their Tesla?

item.title: Sustainability is maturing
item.description: The leaders of tomorrow are growing with it. In 2002, 45% of the world’s top 250 companies reported on sustainability. Today, 96% do. Sustainability metrics that once differentiated companies have become the new baseline. This doesn’t mean sustainability has stalled. Rather, it has matured. As geopolitical and regulatory risks continue progressing, what it means to be a sustainable business is evolving. ...

item.title: Upscale AI in talks to raise at $2B valuation, says report
item.description: AI infrastructure company Upscale AI is reportedly in talks to raise its third funding round since launching just seven months ago.

item.title: Artemis II crew largely sidesteps diversity question about future moon-landing astronauts
item.description: NASA Artemis II astronaut Christina Koch says the future moon-landing Artemis IV crew will probably be diverse without trying.

item.title: Gucci-branded Google smart glasses are coming next year
item.description: Google is reportedly partnering with Gucci to make a pair of AI smart glasses stylish enough people might actually want to wear them. According to Reuters, Gucci parent company Kering is planning to launch the glasses sometime in 2027. Google's first pair of Android XR glasses, "Project Aura," are expected to launch this year. They […]

item.title: Ballmer gives $80 million to NPR, with strings attached
item.description: Connie Ballmer, wife of former Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer and co-founder of the Ballmer Group, has given $80 million to NPR. That's roughly seven years' worth of government funding ($11.2m) after Trump and Congress cut funds for public media, but only a fraction of NPR's full annual budget of $300 million. NPR may still cut […]

item.title: Google Wants to Put Gucci Smart Glasses on Your Face
item.description: Luxury smart glasses, anyone?

item.title: Netflix embraces vertical video with major mobile app update
item.description: Netflix announced on Thursday that it will be launching a redesigned mobile app, which will include a vertical video feed, at the end of April. "This redesign will better reflect our expanding entertainment offering and make it easier for members to engage how and when they want," the company said in its Q1 2026 earnings […]

item.title: Physical Intelligence, a hot robotics startup, says its new robot brain can figure out tasks it was never taught
item.description: The new model, called π0.7, represents what the company describes as an early but meaningful step toward the long-sought goal of a general-purpose robot brain.

item.title: Netflix co-founder Reed Hastings is officially leaving the company
item.description: Netflix co-founder and chairman Reed Hastings plans to leave the company after nearly 30 years. The news comes as part of Netflix's Q1 2026 earnings results released on Thursday, which says Hastings "will not stand for re-election to our Board when his current term expires at the Annual Meeting in June." After co-founding Netflix in […]

item.title: Perplexity brings its Personal Computer AI assistant to Mac
item.description: Perplexity has just released Personal Computer. The software, which is available starting today for Mac, builds on the multi-model orchestration capabilities the company debuted with Perplexity Computer at the end of February. Like Claude Cowork (and, as of today, OpenAI Codex too), it's a suite of computer use agents that can work with your files, apps, connectors and the web to complete complex and "even continuous workflows." Perplexity suggests a few different use cases for Personal Computer, starting with the obvious. “You can ask Personal Computer to read your to-do list,” the company st...

item.title: 2028 candidates will face a new kind of economic anger
item.description: Polls show cratering optimism as working-class voters try to prepare for the seismic changes ahead. Election after election, Democratic strategist James Carville’s maxim, “It’s the economy, stupid!” has held true. But in coming political campaigns, candidates will encounter an especially virulent strain of economic anxiety—driven by artificial intelligence—that is proliferating among lower-wage, working Americans. ...

item.title: '1,000 hp': This record-breaking electric motor packs Tesla Plaid EV power into a beer keg-sized device
item.description: The 12.5kg gas cylinder-sized motor achieves 8 kW per kilogram with a fault-tolerant design, targeting hydrogen hybrid regional aircraft under Project AMBER.

item.title: Disneyland Makes Up for That Han Solo Situation With a Lovely Leia Look
item.description: Disneyland rounds out its trio of original 'Star Wars' heroes coming to 'Galaxy's Edge' with a fun riff on a great Leia design.

item.title: Call of Duty movie arrives on June 30, 2028
item.description: A Call of Duty movie is still happening, but don't hold your breath for it to hit screens any time soon. Today, the popular FPS' social media revealed that the movie's theatrical release date will be June 30, 2028. A film adaptation of the game franchise was first revealed last year, and shortly after, we learned that Taylor Sheridan and Peter Berg would be serving as the producers. The duo, whose past credits include Friday Night Lights and Yellowstone, will also be co-writing the project under Berg's direction. We still haven't heard anything about the cast, or even what era of the long-run...

item.title: Trader Joe’s class action settlement: How to find out if you’re an eligible shopper and claim your money
item.description: The grocery store chain is paying out $7.4 million to customers for printing too many digits of their credit and debit cards on their receipts. Trader Joe’s is settling a class action lawsuit for $7.4 million, after a complaint claimed that the grocery giant printed 10 digits—the first six and last four—of customers’ cards on transaction receipts.

item.title: From the Startup Battlefield stage to the International Space Station: geCKo Materials built a sticky product
item.description: This week on Build Mode, Isabelle Johannessen speaks with Capella Kerst, founder and CEO of geCKo Materials.

item.title: Dark Matter May Be Made of Black Holes From Another Universe
item.description: A model of the cyclic universe suggests that dark matter could be a population of black holes predating the Big Bang.
